source_display: AI as Brain Crutch for ADHD
evidence: I've always been so interested in building out stuff that essentially tries to act as a brain crutch for someone with ADHD - filling gaps between therapy, medication, and coaching

source_display: AI as Brain Crutch for ADHD
evidence: Chris building 360 for executive function support validates the same gap V identifies - bridging therapy, medication, coaching with technology
Chris revealed he's been building his edtech product for almost 1 year, leveraging AI despite being a former special education teacher rather than a developer
Avoid the death by a thousand cuts of endless unpaid pilots. Instead, seek paid pilots with scoped changes and identify design partners willing to work with the product as-is
A cornerstone customer providing reliable revenue creates options - whether to scale, attract others, or seek investment
Product-market fit defined using the Supreme Court's obscenity standard: you know it when you see it - when you have it, you don't think about it; when you don't, you obsess over it constantly
My drive for authenticity is part of the reason why I don't go on social media - it feels like I'd have to be disingenuous and I'm not comfortable with that
Connected through Heather (V's executive function coach who works at Chris's organization)
Vrijen invited Chris to join the Future of Higher Education group (6,000 members globally, ~1,000 in NYC, ~1,000 in Boston) where V serves as NYC co-lead

Context engineering as the foundational practice: not prompting the AI directly, but cultivating the context it uses to generate desired outputs. Three strategic levels: conversation, environment, pipeline
You do not have a technology problem. You have a mental model problem - users collect techniques without understanding how they integrate into effective systems
You can unhobble your AI a lot when you give it access to your files and data... when you give the AI its own home - referencing Anthropic/Claude team concept
Logan analyzed 18 years of LinkedIn data (48 CSV files, 5000+ messages) revealing network mismatch, lost opportunities, 1800% posting-connection correlation
Job security is dead and you need to be building career security for yourself - it has a lot to do with being findable and being visible
Come up with like two to three things that would really delight me... what's LinkedIn sitting on that they should be showing us - ask AI to delight you

source_display: Plan Files as Future of Coding
evidence: Documenting AI conversations in plan files that mix prose and code in stepwise fashion - this is the future of coding: planning systems through narrative structure
Ben Guo introduced Zo Computer as a managed version of Claude Code, giving the AI its own computer (managed cloud server) while handling security and infrastructure complexity
I was traveling to San Francisco and doing this while waiting in line at the airport - exploiting SMS/email AI integration for passive moment productivity
Ben describes Vrijen as really amazing Zo user, kind of one of our power users
Why don't we make a 30 second look at the information we capture via Careerspan? They don't have to wrap their heads around what it is - it's just 'oh shit, I want that information'
The demo is like human data. Like it's a human data oriented demo - positioning Careerspan as a human data company, not a software company
Boom, mic drop for the hiring people. This is opted in candidate data... as things heat up with publicly scraped data, this is a really valuable strategy - referencing Eightfold lawsuit
A deck is not as impactful as watching it in action - V agrees: Yeah, I don't think we should submit the deck
It's kind of a baller move to put the lead list of workforce boards in. That's kind of like just giving value up front - analyzing Springboard's approach
Reference to Eightfold lawsuit as regulatory moment that validates first-party data strategy positioning
Demo structure: show scan, brief interlude showing first-party data collection, show results - evolving positioning from product to data layer

source_display: Sourcing vs Internal Mobility GTM Tracks
evidence: Two separate collateral versions: sourcing/matching for external hiring vs internal mobility for companies with scale like Reliance and Uber
